title: Chapter Ninety-Three: The Main House
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

They arrived at the Namgung family's southern entrance under sheets of rain. Huizhou sprawled before them -- the largest group of halls Yeon-shin had ever seen, walls vast as castle fortifications.

"It's an honour to host such outstanding ones at the main house," Se-jin said, and gestured toward the gate.

Namgung Hwa-shin paused at the threshold. He stared up at the nameplate. "Namgung," he murmured, and the firm spirit he usually carried was not visible.

Yeon-shin watched him and thought: *Can he defeat Namgung Mi? When sorcery loyalty oaths and terrible cold energy bind his every nerve?*

The General Administration Office had spoken of total victory. All opponents eliminated. The Namgung leadership replaced with branch family.

*Something you'd only hear in hero tales.*

---

They bathed. Changed. Were invited to formal dinner.

*Is there martial arts for training thick skin?* Yeon-shin thought, sitting in the neatly decorated hall.

Men and women in luxurious silk surrounded a large oval table. The young master. Namgung Mi. Ten direct-line members, all masters. None showed interest in Hwa-shin. As though illegitimate blood did not exist. Their eyes focused solely on Yeon-shin.

"So you are Lightning Genius." The Namgung Family Head. Middle-aged, distinct features that contained the faces of all three siblings. At his waist hung a bluish sword hilt with words etched in the lantern-light: *Though there be will to surpass the mundane world, cannot cross the Namgung.*

The Southern Wall Sword. Peerless. Forged by the Dwarf Clan.

"Do you know of Ming Cult's rebellion?" the Family Head said. "Our previous Supreme Elder killed their Three Venerables with this blade. It's a treasure bestowed by the Emperor. I'm grateful to you for giving cause to draw it."

"I already heard apologies and thanks from the young master," Yeon-shin replied. *You, however, have not apologized at all.*

The Family Head's wife spoke. "It's amazing you can speak so while seeing the Southern Wall Sword. My husband and children are the walls protecting South Zhili. Desolate Fortress discusses civilian welfare? If that were so, they shouldn't have sent life-and-death challenge letters. How dare you speak of apologies."

"I see why Flourishing Spirit Flower Master's character is insincere."

*Your daughter takes after you.*

Energy waves flared from five or six bodies. Yeon-shin stood.

"Last time you discarded me when I was your ally. Now I'm an enemy who walked defenceless into your inner chambers, and your patience in not killing me -- because you'll lose face -- is remarkable. Perhaps due to the world's eyes on these matches."

"Young master," the wife said coldly. "Don't send that child off prettily."

Se-jin grimaced. "Now it's this son's matter--"

The ceiling exploded.

A purple figure stabbed down into the middle of the table like lightning. Energy waves whirled from the massive body. Dust rose like fog.

An old man rose from within it. White beard to his solar plexus. Blade-sharp eyes. Purple robe with a straight collar.

*Grandfather.*

"Ill-bred spawn!" Ma Yeon-jeok's voice struck in all directions -- Six Harmonies Voice Transmission filling heaven and earth. "What did you just sputter! Things ungrateful under heaven dared discuss my grandson's death! The Fortress Lord did not order the extermination of the Namgung trash family!"

"My wife spoke wrongly," the Family Head said calmly. He alone seemed unshaken.

Ma Yeon-jeok glared. Then suppressed his fury, though his eyes still burned, ready to ignite.

"The martial match date is the Seventh Night. Prepare humbly."

"So be it."

Only then did the old man turn. His eyes met Yeon-shin's and trembled. He twisted his head, checking his grandson's mood. His attitude changed instantly.

"Sorry I couldn't accompany you from the start."

"I heard you received a mission from the Fortress Lord."

"Completed early. When you were entering Huizhou, I saw a powerful blood demon targeting your back. An evil sect Apostle. You needn't worry now. The Elder Council Head is pursuing. He'll bring that demon woman's head."

"Let's move quarters," Ma Yeon-jeok said, wrinkling his nose. "How can one breathe in such sewage?"

Yeon-shin followed him out. Hwa-shin walked at his side and glanced back only once.

---

Days passed. They accepted Namgung Mi's consecutive victory format -- youngest first, winner continuing. That meant Hwa-shin would not fight unless Yeon-shin fell.

The boy rather hoped for that. Sorcery loyalty oaths ran deep in Hwa-shin's consciousness. His dramatic victory was a vain hope.

That day Yeon-shin stayed silently by his companion's side for a long time.

He refined martial arts. The Seventh Night arrived.

*Eternal Blossom Fist -- fourth move. I can complete it in combat.*

He leaned on the bed, reading the Diamond Sutra. *Should not dwell anywhere and thus give rise to mind.* He closed the book and folded the words away inside himself.

"Young Hero Jeong."

Hwa-shin called him with a stiff face. Yeon-shin rose.

*Namgung Mi is not the problem.*

He thought of the young master and his mind burned white-hot. Fear of death, competitive spirit, fascination with Emperor Sword Form -- tangled into one clean thing. Nothing else was visible.

Not the cityscape. Not the Divine Sword Group Leader watching him. Not even the Divine Sword Squad.

Before he knew it, he stood in the middle of Clear Night Valley. The vast arena was full of people -- armies of spectators shouting like thunder.

Namgung Mi faced him with her irritating smile.

"I wanted to know who you were," she said. "But I'll have to examine inside your corpse."

Yeon-shin raised Jeong family dynamic technique to the extreme. He imagined her as the Seventh Apostle.

She stepped with mysterious trigram-based movement techniques, position shifting like smoke. Hard to follow. But then Yeon-shin took one step of Radiant Wing Step. The wind rising from his toes pushed away all energy waves. Every deception lifted like a curtain.

Namgung Mi's swaying body stopped dead.

"Ah--?"

Fear on her fine face. She had underestimated him. The incomplete palm technique he showed before had planted carelessness.

Light gathered in Yeon-shin's hand. He drew the Desolate Sword like a comet and extended Radiant Sword Style in a single unhesitating line through her secret protective energy, through her neck.

Feeling came to the handle. He had completely severed her head.

One sword strike.

"I don't care," the boy said calmly to the corpse. "Whoever you are."

Clear Night Valley fell silent as extinguished embers.

---
